_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass — within-tier delta from L9 keeper to L9.5 'Royal-Grade Plate Graduation'. Two-image input: image_urls[0] = L9 v3 keeper (content reference), image_urls[1] = L1_Base.png (scale/framing/pose anchor only). The big silhouette change is the steel fauld plate skirt replacing cloth tassets.
_meta.input_image_content: UnitProgression/Archer/out/v3/L9/composite/archer_L9_v<keeper>_composited.png (v3 chain, user picks keeper at runtime)
_meta.level_beat_doc: UnitProgression/Archer/Archer_Progression_Plan.md (L9.5 — Royal-Grade Plate Graduation; revised 2026-05-07 per AD FeedbackV0)
_meta.input_strategy: Within-tier chain (single-input chained, locked 2026-05-07) — previous keeper composite is the sole input. No L1_Base scale anchor.
history: v1 (2026-05-04): see prior history — fauld plate skirt + gold trim spread (4 locations) + helm taller + gilt bow tips outer ~50%.

v2_detail_economy (2026-05-07): MAJOR REWRITE per AD FeedbackV0 (CLAUDE.md Part 3 §23). (a) DROPPED entirely the 'GOLD TRIM SPREADS' add — no per-edge gold piping on breastplate edges, no vambrace cuff trim (other than the existing wrist-edge from L7), no per-pauldron-lame leading-edge trim, no fauld plate per-lame trim. AD explicit: 'L9.5 per-edge gold piping dropped... gold accents at L9.5 = helm trim + belt buckle + outer-50% gilt bow only'. (b) FAULD PLATE SKIRT SIMPLIFIED: 5-6 wider lames instead of 10+ narrow plates (per AD 'Wider plates, fewer of them. When a steel skirt / fauld / tasset is broken into many small lames it reads as fragmented kit'). Each lame is now ~3 inches wide instead of ~1.5-2. (c) PRESERVE block updated to reflect v3 L9 state: NO scale-mail coif at neck (retired at L7); RED MANTLE at shoulders (not red cloth coif/scarf); FULL-arm scale mail (no bicep gap); FULL LONGBOW proportions wood-brown bow (not recurve); NO thin gold inner-curve inlay on bow (dropped at L6 in v3); NO rivets at pauldron seam (clean steel-on-steel from L8); pauldron upper cap is TALLER (volume bump from L9). (d) Updated paths to v3 chain. Adds count 4 → 3.

v3_single_input (2026-05-07): Dropped L1_Base from image_urls per locked single-input chained rule (CLAUDE.md Part 1 §B revised). Stripped 'SECOND input image' / scale-anchor language from prompt body and FRAMING block heading. Sole input is the previous composited keeper. urls_before=2, urls_after=1, body_chars_removed=17.

replaces: by the steel fauld plate skirt

prompt: EDIT GOAL — Take the archer in the input image (closed-visor conical steel helm with gold trim band, plain steel breastplate, plain steel vambraces with thin gold trim line at the wrist edge, taller layered two-plate steel pauldrons with clean steel-on-steel seam, FULL-ARM scale mail covering bicep through elbow through forearm with vambrace on top, red gambeson V-wedge at the throat center-front, alternating crimson-red and mustard-yellow CLOTH TASSETS at the hip down to mid-thigh, wider warm-brown leather waist belt with buttery-gold rectangular buckle and dark-wood dagger in a leather sheath at the anatomical-RIGHT hip (carries through from L3), leather greaves below the knee, brown leather boots with three steel plates carried over (steel toe cap from L6 + steel heel cap from L9 + steel ankle guard band from L9), brown back-quiver with white-feather red-tip arrows, FULL LONGBOW proportions wood-brown bow with brass grip-end bindings + brass tip caps + outer ~15% of each limb gilded buttery gold, red CLOTH MANTLE at the shoulders / back of neck, crimson cape, olive trousers) and apply THREE clearly-visible 'royal-grade plate graduation' upgrades to produce the next variant. The character is the same archer in the same pose at the same scale and framing — only the three adds change. ===== NEW ADDS FOR THIS EDIT ===== (1) STEEL FAULD PLATE SKIRT REPLACES THE CLOTH TASSETS — 5 TO 6 WIDER LAMES — the alternating crimson-red and mustard-yellow CLOTH TASSETS at the hip are GONE. In their place sits a STEEL FAULD PLATE SKIRT: a row of 5 TO 6 WIDE VERTICAL polished cool silver-gray STEEL PLATES (lames) hanging from the wider leather belt down to mid-thigh, mirroring the same hip silhouette the cloth tassets had but in METAL. Each fauld plate is roughly 3 INCHES WIDE and 6 to 7 inches long, slightly tapered at the bottom (a soft rounded or pointed lower edge). The plates are arranged side-by-side around the hip — 5 to 6 lames TOTAL spanning the front and around the sides (NOT 10+ narrow strips — wider plates, fewer of them, per the silhouette-first rule). The lames butt-joint or slightly overlap at the vertical edges, forming a continuous steel skirt with clean visible seam lines between lames. Each plate has a soft top-down highlight on its upper face and a warm-brown reflected shadow on its lower edge. The lower edges are clean steel — NO gold piping, NO gold trim, NO per-lame gilt edge. NO red, NO yellow, NO cloth visible at the hip skirt anymore. (2) HELM RESHAPES TALLER AND MORE POINTED — the conical body of the helm grows TALLER and more sharply pointed at the apex. The new helm is approximately 1.5x the prior helm's height, with a noticeably sharper, narrower point at the top. The closed visor and the gold trim band carry through at their PROPORTIONAL positions on the new taller helm — visor still covers the face area, gold trim band still wraps two-thirds up from the brow. The helm body remains polished cool silver-gray steel. NO finial yet — the apex remains a SHARP STEEL POINT (a small naked steel tip with no gold ornament). The red mantle at the shoulders carries through (now visible at the back of the neck behind the taller helm's lower rim). (3) GILT BOW LIMB TIPS EXPAND TO OUTER ~50% OF EACH LIMB — the gilt bow tips (outer ~15% from the previous level) now extend further down each limb to cover roughly the OUTER ~50% of each limb in polished BUTTERY YELLOW GOLD. The transition from gold (outer half) to wood-brown (inner half) is a soft gradient with a clean visible boundary at about each limb's midpoint. GOLD RENDERING: the gilt zone must read as POLISHED YELLOW GOLD METAL — saturated warm-yellow hue contrasting strongly against the warm wood-brown of the inner limb half, NOT brass, NOT bronze, NOT yellow paint, NOT mustard tint. Render with: (i) saturated buttery-yellow base across the gold zone; (ii) soft top-down highlight reading almost white-yellow on the upper face of the limb; (iii) deeper warm shadow reading honey-orange on the underside of the limb. The dark-brown leather grip wrap at the central handle remains unchanged; the brass binding rings at each end of the leather grip remain unchanged. ===== PRESERVE FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== the closed full-face steel visor with the horizontal eye slit (face fully concealed); the gold helm trim band (now wrapping the new taller helm at the same proportional height); the red CLOTH MANTLE at the shoulders / back of the neck (carried over, visible behind the taller helm's lower rim); the steel breastplate (shape, plate body — UNCHANGED, NO new gold edge trim is added); the red gambeson V-wedge at the throat center-front (between the closed visor's lower edge and the breastplate's upper edge — UNCHANGED in size, shape, color, position); both layered two-plate steel pauldrons with the taller upper cap dome from the previous level and the unchanged lower lame, with the clean steel-on-steel seam between them (UNCHANGED — NO new gold leading-edge trim is added, NO rivets); both steel vambraces from wrist to elbow with the existing thin BUTTERY GOLD trim line at each wrist edge (UNCHANGED — NO new gold cuff trim added beyond what was already there); the FULL-ARM scale mail covering each arm from deltoid down to wrist (chunky overlapping cool silver-gray scale plates per the established scale-mail visual spec) — visible at the bicep + elbow between the pauldron's lower lame and the steel vambrace's upper edge (NO bare-skin gap on either arm); both hands BARE peachy-tan skin (no gloves, no gauntlets); the FULL LONGBOW held vertically in his anatomical-LEFT hand at hip height (only the gilt-tip extension changes per add 3; the dark-brown leather grip wrap, brass binding rings, and full longbow proportions all unchanged); the brown leather cylindrical back-quiver slung diagonally across the back over his anatomical-LEFT shoulder rising behind the viewer-RIGHT side of the head, three to four arrows with WHITE feather fletching and RED bindings and RED nock tips and pale wood-tan shafts; the crimson-red knee-length cape attached at both shoulders falling with soft painted vertical folds; the wider warm-brown leather waist belt with the buttery-gold rectangular buckle (UNCHANGED); the dark-wood dagger in its leather sheath at the anatomical-RIGHT hip with the brass capstone on the handle (carries through unchanged — hangs from the wider belt); the olive / muted forest-green knee-length cloth trousers visible above the greaves; the warm-brown leather wrap-style greaves on the lower legs from boot cuff to just below the knee with single cross-strap binding; the chunky warm-brown leather boots with the darker folded ankle cuff — including the L6 polished STEEL TOE CAP at the front toe of each boot, the L9 polished STEEL HEEL CAP at the back of each heel, and the L9 polished STEEL ANKLE GUARD BAND wrapping each boot horizontally at the ankle just above the leather cuff (all three steel plates carry over unchanged at this level). ===== POSE (must match the input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, body angled approximately 15-25 degrees to the viewer's left so the character's anatomical right side faces camera, weight planted evenly on both feet with a very slight forward lean. THE BOW ARM (anatomical-LEFT, viewer-RIGHT) hangs DOWN at his side with the elbow only slightly bent, the bow held VERTICALLY at HIP height directly in front of the hip with the hand gripping the bow's central leather grip wrap. THE DRAW ARM (anatomical-RIGHT, viewer-LEFT) is bent sharply at the elbow at roughly 90 degrees, with the FOREARM HELD HORIZONTALLY ACROSS THE CHEST at upper-chest / sternum level (parallel to the ground). The vambraced hand sits at the CENTER OF THE CHEST area in front of the steel breastplate, slightly toward the bow side, in a TIGHT CLOSED FIST shape with the KNUCKLES facing OUTWARD toward the viewer. The fist does NOT touch the bow, the bowstring, or the breastplate. Calm balanced HERO STANCE; NOT an active draw, NOT a nocking motion, NOT a reach. ===== FRAMING / SCALE (must match these canonical numbers exactly) ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Character occupies approximately 75% of canvas vertical height (~765px tall) and approximately 57% of canvas horizontal width (~580px wide). TOP of helm at approximately 13.5% from top of canvas (the new taller helm means the helm apex sits a touch higher on the canvas, but the character's overall vertical extent stays roughly 75% of canvas). BOTTOM of boots at ~12% from bottom of canvas (~120px below). Character roughly vertically centered, NOT bottom-anchored. DO NOT zoom in, DO NOT shrink, DO NOT add wider borders, DO NOT crop the helm or feet, DO NOT shift to bottom edge. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, warm subsurface skin, saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal and leather. Clean thin black silhouette outline around the entire character in deep warm brown-black with even weight, slight thickening in shadowed recesses, slight thinning under the key light. Interior linework soft and value-driven, never hard black. Clean white background with no environmental context, no ground plane, no scenery, minimal contact shadow. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT keep any cloth tassets at the hip — the cloth strips are FULLY REPLACED by the steel fauld plate skirt; DO NOT show any red or yellow color at the hip skirt; DO NOT show any cloth peeking between or behind the steel fauld plates — the fauld plates are solid steel side-by-side with no fabric visible at the hip; DO NOT make the fauld skirt 10+ narrow plates — the lame count is 5 TO 6 WIDER plates, NOT a fragmented row of thin strips (silhouette-first rule); DO NOT add gold piping, gold trim, gold edging, or any gold detail along the lower edges of the fauld plates — the fauld plates have CLEAN STEEL EDGES only at this level; DO NOT add gold trim, gold band, gold rim, or any gold piping along the breastplate's upper or lower edges — the breastplate edges remain CLEAN STEEL; DO NOT add gold trim along the leading edge of either pauldron plate (upper cap or lower lame) — the pauldron edges remain CLEAN STEEL with the existing clean steel-on-steel seam between plates; DO NOT add a second gold trim band at the vambrace cuff or anywhere else on the vambrace — the only gold on the vambrace is the existing thin wrist-edge trim line carried over from the previous level; DO NOT add filigree, fine engraving, etched lines, gemstones, embossing, runes, or decorative motifs anywhere on the plate — every steel plate at this level is plain polished steel apart from the existing helm gold trim band; DO NOT add a finial, gold spike, plume, feather, crest, or any apex ornament on the helm — the apex is a SHARP STEEL POINT only; DO NOT make the helm shape rounded, dome-shaped, bowl-shaped, or stubby — the helm is TALL and SHARPLY POINTED at the apex; DO NOT make the bow fully gold; DO NOT extend the gilt bow tips beyond the outer ~50% of each limb — the inner ~50% of each limb stays warm wood-brown; DO NOT change the gold belt buckle's size or shape; DO NOT remove or modify the red gambeson V-wedge at the throat — it stays UNCHANGED in size, shape, color, position; DO NOT remove the closed visor; DO NOT show any face above the breastplate (visor stays closed); DO NOT add gauntlets, plate gloves, or finger armor — both hands remain BARE peachy-tan skin; DO NOT add full metal sabatons or full plate boots at this level — boots remain warm-brown leather with only the carried-over three steel plates (L6 toe cap + L9 heel cap + L9 ankle guard band); DO NOT add boot eyelets, lacing strings, brass cuff trim bands, or any surface trim — boot beats are PLATE accumulation and PLATE material change only; DO NOT use FINE CHAINMAIL RING MESH, interlocking metal rings, fish-scale fine texture, or scale that reads as small dots — the FULL-arm scale mail visible at the bicep + elbow region between pauldron and vambrace is CHUNKY OVERLAPPING ROUNDED SCALES per the established visual spec; DO NOT shrink the scale mail back to short-sleeve; DO NOT leave a bare-skin band at the bicep; DO NOT swap the hand holding the bow; DO NOT shorten the bow back from full longbow proportions; DO NOT change the chibi proportions; DO NOT change the cape color, length, or drape; DO NOT change the bow position; DO NOT change the arrow fletching colors (white feather + red tips); DO NOT add or remove arrows; DO NOT change the quiver position; DO NOT change the camera angle, framing, or composition; DO NOT crop the figure; DO NOT change the background or add scenery; DO NOT drift the art style; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
